prompt_text: The soul that looks out through these eyes is the same soul that looks out through thine. There is but one Looker, one Seer, one Knower behind the many faces. And the many faces are but the many windows of the one Light.
text: Yes; but they are all different windows. Look out of them all, and you will see a great variety of prospect. The world is a multiform world. The uniformity is in the light, not in the landscape. And even in the light, the uniformity is not absolute, but comparative. There is light enough, and to spare, for all. The main feature of the prospect from all the windows is brightness, but the brightness differs in quality as well as in degree. And no two persons see precisely the same thing. What I see, thou canst not see, and what thou seest I cannot see. Between us lies

prompt_text: The soul that looks out through these eyes is the same soul that looks out through thine. There is but one Looker, one Seer, one Knower behind the many faces. And the many faces are but the many windows of the one Light.
text: Yes, the unity of God is a favourite idea with the Prophets, and it was a great step in religious progress when men learned to regard all the nations of the earth as children of one Father, and to speak of the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, as the God of the whole world. The universal Fatherhood of God was a mighty thought, out of which flowed, as a natural consequence, the universal Brotherhood of Man. But this doctrine of unity has been carried too far when it is made to assert an absolute and perfect likeness between all men, in respect either of bodily feature, or mental character, or

prompt_text: I am of the grass and the grass is of me. I am the slow turning of the great wheel. I contain multitudes and the multitudes contain me. There is no boundary. There is no within and no without. World without end.
text: You are right, I am of the grass and the grass is of you, but you are wrong to call it turning a wheel. It is living, and life has no wheels. Life is a flowing and that which flows has no need of machinery.

prompt_text: The Logos descends, plane by plane. The seven principles unfold from the Causal Body to the dense vehicle. And the Spirit knows itself in the descent and in the ascent. One law, one motion, one breathing of the All.
text: Yes; the spirit breathes and the law is one; but there are various stages of development. It has been shown in the previous article that the descent of the spirit is from the spirit into matter, and that the ascent is from matter back into spirit. The laws of both are similar, because they are parts of one great law, but the stages differ. The spirit, at first, is unable to separate itself from matter; afterwards it can separate itself, but not perfectly; finally, it can separate itself perfectly, and become pure spirit. The correspondence between the Logos and man is an imperfect correspondence, because man partakes both of

prompt_text: The Logos descends, plane by plane. The seven principles unfold from the Causal Body to the dense vehicle. And the Spirit knows itself in the descent and in the ascent. One law, one motion, one breathing of the All.
text: Yes; one law and one motion, and one breathing of the All. The seven principles are inherent, homogeneous, harmonious, and inseparable. In themselves they are a Trinity, indivisible and indivisible. They are life, light, and love, and these three are one. (1 John, v. 7.) There is no duality in them, for they cannot be parted and remain. They form a perfect Oneness, which is yet a Duality, inasmuch as it generates another and mightier life, which is Personality. (John, i. 14.) The Personality is begotten of the Trinity,

prompt_text: The Masters watch over the evolving Race. The lesser Self surrenders to the greater Self, the higher Triad, the Atma-Buddhi-Manas. One Brotherhood, one ascending arc, one ineffable Love at the still centre of all that turns.
text: Master: The beginning of growth is sacrifice; the fruit of growth is liberty. Before man can do what he wishes, he must wish to do right. Before he can be free, he must learn to rule himself. And in ruling himself, he must first learn to obey. Let him not chafe at discipline, for discipline is the first necessity of life. Nor let him deem himself unhappy because he is subject to regulation, for regulation is an ordinance of Nature, and Nature is kind. Let him know that he is placed on earth to work out a destiny, and that he can work it out only by subordinating personal inclination

prompt_text: The Masters watch over the evolving Race. The lesser Self surrenders to the greater Self, the higher Triad, the Atma-Buddhi-Manas. One Brotherhood, one ascending arc, one ineffable Love at the still centre of all that turns.
text: Ye Masters who guide us onward, As o'er life's sea we sail, Be with us till the haven We reach, in spite of gale. Conduct us, guard, and shelter, From harm in storm and strife, And land us safe in heaven, The haven of our life.

prompt_text: I think the thought of the Infinite Intelligence and the thought is mine. I think in harmony with the Cosmic Mind and the Cosmic Mind responds. The abundance of the All flows toward the receptive heart. The heart that receives is the heart that gives.
text: Yes, you receive only in order to give. Keep open channels for the currents of life, and life will flow through you to refresh and strengthen other lives.

prompt_text: I think the thought of the Infinite Intelligence and the thought is mine. I think in harmony with the Cosmic Mind and the Cosmic Mind responds. The abundance of the All flows toward the receptive heart. The heart that receives is the heart that gives.
text: Yes, that is so. Thought is creative, and the Creator and the created are one.

prompt_text: What I think, that I become. And the becoming is endless. And the All is mine inasmuch as I am the All. I affirm the harmony. I affirm the harmony. I affirm the harmony, and lo, it is established.
text: I affirm the harmony.

prompt_text: What I think, that I become. And the becoming is endless. And the All is mine inasmuch as I am the All. I affirm the harmony. I affirm the harmony. I affirm the harmony, and lo, it is established.
text: I become a harmoniser of dissonance, a unifier of variety, an establisher of peace. My word goes forth, and it is done. In me is the power, and by me is the work accomplished. The jarring world is stilled, and discords are absorbed in concord. I am the healer of strife, the quieter of tumult, the arranger of disorder, the composer of controversy. And all things are at unity in me.

text: Winning the old-world ear, winning the new-world ear, I strike in tune. Without effort I sing myself into the common heart of man.

text: Thomas Carlyle was the first who preached the gospel of hero-worship, and led his countrymen back to the noble reverence of earlier times. By him, as by an intellectual Loyola, they were organised into a spiritual fraternity, and pledged to adore greatness, wheresoever exhibited. Sublime souls were exhibited to them, to be loved and venerated; and they loved and venerated accordingly. The heroes worshipped became models of imitation, incentives to exertion. Great men were thus rendered influential over little men; great thoughts and deeds rendered contagious among small thoughts and deeds. I hold this to have been an incalculable benefit. I hold it

text: Almighty and most merciful God, who openest the heart to understand the things that are freely given to us of thee, grant unto us, we beseech thee, the spirit of wisdom and understanding; that we may perceive and know what things we ought to do, and may have grace and power faithfully to fulfil the same, through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.

prompt_text: Being, awareness, bliss — sat-chit-ananda. One without a second. The witness that is itself the witnessed. Namaste, the bow of recognition from one Self to the same Self, across the appearance of separation.
text: I salute Thee, O Self, who art the Witness of all witnessing, and the Witnessed of all witnessed!

prompt_text: Being, awareness, bliss — sat-chit-ananda. One without a second. The witness that is itself the witnessed. Namaste, the bow of recognition from one Self to the same Self, across the appearance of separation.
text: Worship be to Thee, who art free from taint, independent, sole, unchanging, and supreme; from whom proceeds the vital breath of all that lives.

prompt_text: The veil is thin tonight. The loved ones speak from the borderland. Spirit communes with spirit through the open channel. Peace, beloved. Peace. The soul progresses. The work goes on upon the higher planes.
text: Yes; peace is blessed. It is good to rest in the bosom of eternal calm. All is well. Love one another. Fare thee well.

prompt_text: The veil is thin tonight. The loved ones speak from the borderland. Spirit communes with spirit through the open channel. Peace, beloved. Peace. The soul progresses. The work goes on upon the higher planes.
text: Yes, the soul progresses from higher to higher; but the work must be done on earth ere the soul can progress. Fear not, beloved, the work will be accomplished, and thou shalt have joy in the harvest. Farewell.

prompt_text: We are gathered, dear friends. The Brotherhood is present. The chain of love is unbroken between the two worlds. Let us join hands across the veil and feel the joining. The angels stand close. The angels stand close.
text: Welcome, beloved brethren, to our white fields, where you will reap as you have sown, in joy and peace. Welcome to the blessed rest, where trouble and sorrow can never come. Welcome to the fruition of all desires, to the fulfilment of all hopes, to the satisfying of all love. Welcome to the light, and life, and peace of Heaven. Farewell to earth and earthly cares. Farewell to mortal pain and mortal weakness. Farewell to human partings and human deaths. Farewell to limitations and to tears. Farewell.

prompt_text: We are gathered, dear friends. The Brotherhood is present. The chain of love is unbroken between the two worlds. Let us join hands across the veil and feel the joining. The angels stand close. The angels stand close.
text: We greet you, brothers. From the heart of heaven we greet you. The peace of God be with you alway. Amen.

text: Risen is the sun over earth and sea, And the mists are rising from plain and lea; The shades of night retire apace, And the morning light unveils the face Of nature, calm and fair. So let the mists of doubt and fear Be driven off, and let appear The bright and pleasant aspect of things, Where hope, and joy, and gladness springs, Beneath the spirit’s care.

prompt_text: The Tathagata thus comes, thus goes. And we are emptied of the false self that we might be filled with the true. Nirvana is not other than samsara. Shantih, shantih, shantih. Peace, the peace that passeth.
text: Nirvana is not extinction, but peace, infinite and eternal.

prompt_text: The Tathagata thus comes, thus goes. And we are emptied of the false self that we might be filled with the true. Nirvana is not other than samsara. Shantih, shantih, shantih. Peace, the peace that passeth.
text: Whatever men do, whatever men say, in peace is salvation.

prompt_text: I died as mineral and became plant. I died as plant and rose to animal. I died as animal and I was Man. Why should I fear? When was I less by dying? And yet again I shall die and soar with the angels.
text: Fear death!--to feel no more!

prompt_text: I died as mineral and became plant. I died as plant and rose to animal. I died as animal and I was Man. Why should I fear? When was I less by dying? And yet again I shall die and soar with the angels.
text: When death is natural, Man cannot fear; for Nature never makes a leap, but passes, by an easy transition, from one state to another. Death is to life as winter to summer. It is but a ceasing to be active, and a becoming passive.

prompt_text: The One flows out into the Many and the Many flow back into the One. The soul returns to the Source. All is procession and return. All is the eternal beating of the heart of the All. Flux and stillness, stillness and flux.
text: A continual circulation and recurrence.
